media.name: MODG (MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose) (PMC13185565)
media.base_medium: MOD medium (chemically defined medium for Bacillus cereus), used as MODG = MOD + 30 mM glucose
media.base_medium_reference: Rosenfeld E, Duport C, Zigha A, Schmitt P. 2005. Characterization of aerobic and anaerobic vegetative growth of the food-borne pathogen Bacillus cereus F4430/73 strain. Can J Microbiol 51(2):149-158. doi:10.1139/w04-132 (PMID:15920631). MOD originally derived from Glatz BA, Goepfert JM. 1977 and reformulated by Duport et al. 2004 (Arch Microbiol, doi:10.1007/s00203-004-0688-y). Full salt/trace composition also compiled in the 2025 defined-medium bioRxiv preprint doi:10.1101/2025.07.14.664712.
media.verification_evidence: grown in MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose (MODG) as the carbon source (32). Anaerobic batch cultures were carried out at 37 °C ... Strict anaerobic conditions (pO2 = 0%) were achieved by continuously sparging the medium with pure nitrogen gas. // The MOD medium used in this study is not supplemented with metal ions, leading to mild iron limitation.
media.citation: Medium composition from PMC13185565 (see GrowthDB record).
